steps.title: Solve the system by setting equations equal
steps.content: At intersection points, both equations are satisfied simultaneously. Set $x^2 - 1 = 3$: $$x^2 - 1 = 3$$ $$x^2 = 4$$ $$x = \pm 2$$

steps.title: Write the intersection points
steps.content: Both intersection points lie on the line $y = 3$. With $x = 2$ and $x = -2$, the coordinates are $(2, 3)$ and $(-2, 3)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
